title: Worker-rights domain pack (wage theft, overtime, misclassification, safety, retaliation)
context: Worker rights — wage theft, unpaid overtime, misclassification, unsafe conditions, retaliation — represent the second planned domain for Know-Your-Rights after the tenant-rights pilot is stable and delivered (M6). Like the tenant-rights content, worker-rights explainers must be general legal information (plain-language summaries of what the law of the named jurisdiction says), not individualized legal advice. Every claim must be primary-source-cited to the named jurisdiction's statutes, regulations, or official agency guidance. A licensed attorney admitted in the jurisdiction and experienced in employment/labor law must sign off before delivery. This task is backlog: not started until M6 is complete.
objective: Draft and iterate to final form the plain-language worker-rights explainer set for a confirmed second jurisdiction (or the same pilot jurisdiction), covering wage theft, overtime rules, worker misclassification, workplace safety rights, and retaliation protections. All content is general legal information — summaries of what the law says — not individualized advice. Every claim is primary-source-cited, lint-clean, and attorney-signed-off before delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Five topic explainers produced for the confirmed jurisdiction: wage theft and minimum wage, overtime rules (including exemptions), worker misclassification (employee vs contractor), workplace safety rights, and retaliation protections for workers who complain

Every explainer frames content as general legal information — plain-language summaries of what the law of the named jurisdiction says — not individualized legal advice or case-specific recommendations
Every legal claim is primary-source-cited to the named jurisdiction (statute, regulation, official agency guidance); source reuse terms verified per the source-vetting process from task 006
All explainers pass the full content-lint suite (task 004): non-advice, neutrality, jurisdiction-label, citation-coverage, and readability (reading level at or below grade 8)
Carries the 'not legal advice' banner and route-to-help block (worker center, wage claim agency, legal aid) on every page
Licensed-attorney sign-off recorded (admitted in the jurisdiction, experienced in employment/labor law) before delivery; sign-off version-scoped
Sequenced after M6 is complete and the tenant-rights pilot is stable and delivered
output: Five plain-language worker-rights explainers for the confirmed jurisdiction — schema-valid, primary-source-cited, lint-clean, carrying the 'not legal advice' banner and route-to-help block, and licensed-attorney-signed-off — expanding the Know-Your-Rights commons to the worker-rights domain.
